name: btrfs
explanation: The Btrfs check has no required configuration fields and no network endpoint. It enumerates `psutil.disk_partitions()` on the Agent host, filters those whose `fstype == 'btrfs'`, and issues `BTRFS_IOC_SPACE_INFO` / `BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO` / `BTRFS_IOC_DEV_INFO` ioctls against each mountpoint via a directory file descriptor. There is no host/port/URL/credential to discover. Auto-config is therefore trivial: emit the integration with its default empty instance whenever the Agent host has at least one mounted btrfs filesystem (or even unconditionally — the check raises only if zero btrfs devices are present). Discovery is a host-local presence check ("is any filesystem of type btrfs mounted?"), analogous to pattern H/L but with no binary or network involved.
notes: Classified as generic because the trigger is universal (presence of a btrfs mount on the host) and no per-instance input is needed. Method `other` because there is no network probe — discovery is via the host's mount table. The Agent process must run with permissions to open the mountpoint and issue the ioctls (typically root or CAP_SYS_ADMIN), which is an environmental constraint rather than a credential.
